title: Two-phase validate-then-write pattern for contact atomicity should be defended
description: ContactPatchOp enum + Phase 1 collect / Phase 2 write is the correct structure for RFC 8620 §7.1 input-error atomicity. A future contributor might 'simplify' this back to interleaved validate-and-write (like the old code) on the grounds that the enum adds indirection. That would be wrong: the enum exists to guarantee no store write happens if any field fails validation. The same pattern is used for SimplePropPatch in space_set; its presence in both places is intentional and should be preserved.
close_reason: decision recorded in beads, no code action needed
